title: After-Action Report — Custom League Editing: Coordinate Weather, City Catalogue, Live Ballpark Editor, League Renaming
scope: The user wanted to push league/team customization further so they can build "weirder and more expansive" leagues.

rationale.item: "I want to have a better way to edit league names and team names if I want to … create more custom leagues that I can set the geography of teams and stuff."
"Add more Finnish cities too and far more metro and micropolitan markets so I can get weirder and more expansive."
"Weather geography just would pick the closest city to them, it doesnt need to be exact."
"Also this would let me make ballpark dimensions on the fly so I can get weird then too in real time if i want to."

delegation_events.decision: All four capabilities wanted; edits allowed at any time (played games keep stamped weather; unplayed home games re-roll)
delegation_events.quote: A scoping question confirmed all four capabilities were wanted, and that edits should be allowed **at any time** (played games keep their stamped weather; unplayed home games re-roll).

actions.description: Coordinate-based nearest-city weather: lat/lon columns on teams (schema + idempotent ALTER migration), seed_league persists coords, weather.py gains coordinate gazetteer (_BASE_COORDS, _EXTRA_CITIES, _CLIMATE_ANCHORS), nearest_city / archetype_for_coords / city_gazetteer, draw_weather lat/lon params (backward compatible, RNG-consumption unchanged); coords flow into schedule.py and playoffs.py weather draws
City catalogue grown from ~150 to 362 cities with coordinates + archetype: ~40 more Finnish towns (all subarctic), ~120 US metro/micropolitan markets tagged by climate region, global anchor spread

Live ballpark editor on /team/<id>/edit: park name, all 12 shapes, six dimensions, HR/hits park factors, 25-entry quirk checkbox list (shape-fit hints shown but not enforced), Re-roll ballpark button; new public helpers get_park_shapes, get_quirk_catalog, get_park_shape_meta, quirk_meta, roll_park

Relocation with a coordinate-aware city picker: datalist of all 362 gazetteer cities, auto-fill lat/lon + archetype preview, manual lat/lon override; server-side resolution order explicit lat/lon → gazetteer match → existing team coords
Rename leagues & divisions: new /league/edit page linked via a Rename button; display-only renames of league/division columns on teams

risk_posture: **Backward compatibility.** `draw_weather`'s new params default to `None`; the name-lookup path and RNG sequence are unchanged, so existing seeds reproduce bit-for-bit.
Renaming is **display-only** — it updates the `league` / `division` columns on `teams`; records, rosters, and the schedule are untouched.
**Quirks are not shape-gated in the editor.** Generation still gates quirks by shape, but manual editing intentionally allows any combination — the whole point was to "get weird."
**League renaming is display-only and independent per name.** Avoids magic prefix-rewriting that could clobber a division the user is also renaming in the same submit.

validation_claims.claim: End-to-end pipeline on a fresh temp DB: seed → schedule → relocate to Helsinki → reshape park → re-roll → rename leagues/divisions; teams carry coords, games stamp weather, all writes verified
validation_claims.check_method: Repeat the pipeline on a temp DB

validation_claims.claim: Web layer via Flask's test client: /team/<id>/edit and /league/edit GET (200), save/re-roll/rename POST (302) with DB assertions
Edge cases: legacy row with NULL coords + empty park renders and saves; "Nuuk GRL" with manual coordinates persists and resolves to the nearest anchor
nearest_city spot-checks: Seinäjoki → subarctic, Boise → arid_steppe, Miami → tropical, Lapland point → Sodankylä/subarctic, Tokyo point → subtropical_humid
Test suite matches the pristine baseline (120 passed, 7 skipped); two pre-existing issues reproduce identically on the unchanged tree
validation_claims.check_method: Run the full suite and compare to baseline

non_actions.reason: the work was done in a headless container, so the editor UI was exercised via the Flask test client and DB assertions rather than a live browser session

residual_risks: Editor UI not exercised in a real browser
open_hooks: After renaming a league (AL → Atlantic) the user may also want to rename matching divisions independently (AL West → Atlantic West)
section_headers_original: After-Action Report — Custom League Editing: Coordinate Weather, City Catalogue, Live Ballpark Editor, League Renaming
4. Relocation with a coordinate-aware city picker
unmapped_content: Header metadata: Branch claude/custom-league-editing-Vp67c; commit 15eda4e. Starting state: teams from o27v2/data/teams_database.json (86 franchises with name/city/lat/lon/level), coordinates used once at seed time then discarded; weather mapped city → archetype by exact name match with trailing country-code fallback, unknown cities silently fell back to continental_warm (~150 world cities, 20 Finnish); team editor edited name/abbrev/city as free text; ballpark dimensions rolled once at seed and not editable; league/division names fixed at seed. Design notes: squared equirectangular distance, not haversine — monotone in true distance so nearest-neighbour ranking is identical, and the user explicitly said exactness doesn't matter; single source of truth for climate — the gazetteer lives in the engine (weather.py) so the lower-layer engine stays self-contained and the editor reads its pick-list from the same place the weather draw does. Files-touched table enumerates per-file changes including app.py helpers _team_climate, _parse_park_dims, _reroll_weather_for_team. Pre-existing test issues: test_weather_calibration K-rate envelope (sampling-flaky) and test_stat_invariants errors (need a DB setup absent in this container).
